mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by positive index returns and fails if cuts occur without equity repricing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the hit rate is below 55% in easing periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals labor-market deterioration and higher recession probability, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is falsified if unemployment spikes do not precede negative equity performance.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if average forward return after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate, household spending and payroll income are jointly strengthening, implying better near-term revenue growth for large-cap equities and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if strong sales growth occurs without subsequent index gains.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-acceleration state does not outperform either single proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if 60d returns are not positive in the top-quartile joint-growth state.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO reading indicates weakening industrial activity and lower cyclical earnings momentum, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The pattern is falsified if industrial contraction is not followed by equity underperformance.
failure_condition: Reject if negative monthly changes in INDPRO are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above trend signals housing-cycle acceleration and stronger downstream demand, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The hypothesis fails if housing starts break higher without a subsequent broad-market advance.
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 60d SPX returns are not greater than zero on average, or if the trigger has no predictive lift versus the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, policy stays restrictive and real rates stay tight, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The claim fails if high inflation with tight policy does not coincide with weaker equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is rising and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the high-inflation and high-rate state does not produce below-zero 20d SPX returns on average, or if interaction tests show no incremental effect beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor market, a rising UNRATE coupled with slowing PAYEMS growth signals weakening income and demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is specific to abrupt deteriorations and should vanish when payroll growth remains firm.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp while PAYEMS growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events fail to produce negative 5d SPX returns, or if the combined condition does not outperform UNRATE-only and PAYEMS-only signals.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in GDPC1 indicates real demand growth and improving earnings breadth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is most reliable when output growth is broad-based rather than driven by one-off quarterly noise.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth does not correspond to positive 60d SPX returns over rolling walk-forward windows, or if the directional hit rate falls below 55%.
